summary: Auth0-impersonating loader package pulls unrelated sibling dependency at require-time
details: Package name impersonates the Auth0 vendor namespace but is published by 'OpenSource Contributor', not Auth0. The entire main entry (index.js) is a single `require('auth0-templates-utils')` wrapped in try/catch followed by a console message; the package contains no real functionality. Its sole effect is to pull `auth0-templates-utils` (^1.0.3) into the installer's dependency graph and load it at require-time. The combination of vendor-namespace impersonation, placeholder author metadata ('OpenSource Contributor'), zero real functionality, and sole purpose of loading an unrelated sibling package is the classic typosquat-loader / namespace-abuse pattern: the harm payload lives in the sibling, while this package serves as the lure that pulls it in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
